title: sdnotify play kube policies: podman container wait, hangs
body:
```
[+1368s] not ok 310 sdnotify : play kube - with policies
...
$ podman container inspect test_pod-a --format {{.Config.SdNotifySocket}}
/var/tmp/-podman-notify-proxy.sock54032352
$ podman logs test_pod-a
/run/notify/notify.sock
READY
$ podman exec test_pod-a /bin/touch /stop
$ podman container inspect 9cffc8377ee1-service --format {{.State.ConmonPid}}
98526
$ podman container wait test_pod-a
timeout: sending signal TERM to command ?/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/bin/podman?
[ rc=124 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
*** TIMED OUT ***
```

So far, f36 only (both amd64 and aarch64), root and rootless.

comments.body: Will take a look, thanks!
Possibly related to #16062?
I recently attempted to fix some issues down there.  @edsantiago, do you know when the flake happened for the _first_ time?

> I recently attempted to fix some issues down there. @edsantiago, do you know when the flake happened for the _first_ time?

Just saw it at the bottom "Looks like Sept 9 is the first logged instance."

I found one potential race that https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16118 addresses.  The potential race looks extreeeemely narrow to me, so I am not sure whether the PR fixes this issue.

Let's keep an eye open and please let me know if the flakes pops up again after https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16118 merges.

Closing as https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16118 merged.  I am not 100 percent sure it fixes the flake but it's the only potential source for the flake I could spot so far.  Let's reopen in case it continues.

Reopening: [this is still happening](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5764449016479744/html/sys-podman-fedora-36-rootless-host.log.html) (and yes, I confirmed that the PR in question is forked from a `main` that includes #16118).
Can we close https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16246 as a duplicate, or is there a need to track it separately?

Seen also in [fedora gating tests](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/bececdc4-9adf-4e74-ae83-56fbd52f24db/work-tests.ymlXe1Zth/tests-BAqFjn/test.podman-root-cgroupsv2.bats.log). [Edit: yes, dup. I was hunting for the log link before closing that one]
Thanks, @edsantiago!  I will take a look at this one.  A stubborn issue!
Reopening as I don't expect https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16284 to fix the underlying issue.  It may help to resolve the timeout but should fail.
@edsantiago have you seen this one flake recently?

Help is on the way in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16709.  This has the highest priority for me this week.  Currently refining tests, so I am hopeful the flake will be buried this week.
@edsantiago any indications of a flake after the merge?

In the last ten minutes, no :-)

Patience, grasshopper. Data collection takes time. I'll let you know late December.

> In the last ten minutes, no :-)

GitHub claims it to be 13 hours :^)

> Patience, grasshopper. Data collection takes time. I'll let you know late December.

Thanks! I intended to be pro-active; it has been quite a flake/ride.

Two flakes on December 14 (after the Dec 8 merge). Both on PR 16781 which, if I'm gitting correctly, was parented on a Dec 7 commit that did not include #16709. So I think we're good. Thank you @vrothberg!
